channel.title: Pratik Dhanave — Blog
channel.description: Long-form writing on multi-agent AI, medical AI governance, HIPAA-aware architecture, and cloud-native systems.

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop: An Approval Gate on Durable State
item.description: Series finale, Lesson 7: a sensitive action pauses for human approval, where suspension is just a Lesson 2 checkpoint marked awaiting_approval, the deadline is checked first so a late yes is void, and the action must be idempotent.

item.title: Hierarchical Supervision: Bounded Fan-Out, Ordered Fan-In, Fault Isolation
item.description: Lesson 6: bounded fan-out behind a semaphore, ordered fan-in via a pre-sized results slice, and per-worker fault isolation so one sub-agent panicking becomes one failed result instead of crashing the whole run.

item.title: Orchestration and Handoff: Routing Intent to a Specialist
item.description: Lesson 5: a triage router first-matches a keyword to hand intent to a specialist, and the exact point a substring table stops being able to think.

item.title: Advanced Memory: Threads, Keyword Retrieval, and Lossy Summarization
item.description: Lesson 4: memory is three stores, not one — an append-only thread, a keyword knowledge index, and a lossy first-and-last summarizer — and an honest account of where each local stand-in leaks against Azure.

item.title: Secure Sandboxing: Running Agent-Written Code Behind a Timeout
item.description: Lesson 3: run agent-written code behind a hard timeout with exec.CommandContext, distinguish OK from TimedOut, and face the leak — a subprocess is not a security boundary.

item.title: Durable Execution: Checkpoint Every Step, Resume After a Crash
item.description: Lesson 2: a workflow that checkpoints after every step and resumes from the last one after a crash, why at-least-once execution forces idempotent steps, and the atomic-rename store that survives a killed process.

item.title: The Agent Harness: guardrails as middleware around the model
item.description: Lesson 1: why the input guardrail is a hard block rather than flag-and-pass, why it counts runes instead of bytes, and how a plain net/http handler wraps the (stubbed) model call so it tests with httptest.

item.title: AG-UI State Management: The Server
item.description: The server side of AG-UI state management: middleware emits a DataContent state snapshot from the model's JSON so the client can adopt shared state across turns.

item.title: Harness Engineering in Go: build the harness, then let Azure supply it
item.description: Seven patterns that turn a bare model call into production agent infrastructure, each written first as offline Go behind an interface (the seam) so the leap to Azure is a swap, not a rewrite.

item.title: AG-UI Human-in-the-Loop: The Server
item.description: The server side of AG-UI human-in-the-loop: gate a tool with tool.ApprovalRequiredFunc so the server suspends the run and resumes on the client's decision.

item.title: AG-UI Frontend Tools: The Server
item.description: The server side of AG-UI frontend tools: set DisableFuncAutoCall so the server forwards tool calls to the client to execute and awaits the result.

item.title: Hosting and the Capstone App — MAF in Go
item.description: Wrap Go agents in A2A, MCP, and AG-UI transports, then run the end-to-end client/server capstone where a host agent uses remote specialists as tools.

item.title: AG-UI Backend Tools: The Server
item.description: The server side of AG-UI backend tools: attach server-owned function tools to the Foundry agent so the tool round-trip stays entirely server-side.

item.title: Advanced Workflows — MAF in Go
item.description: Durable MAF workflows in Go: checkpoint and rehydrate a fresh graph, pause on a RequestPort for a human, nest sub-workflows, and coordinate via scoped shared state.

item.title: AG-UI Getting Started: The Server
item.description: The AG-UI server half: wrap a Foundry agent in aguiprovider.NewJSONHTTPHandler and serve it over HTTP/SSE for any AG-UI client to drive.

item.title: Orchestration Patterns — MAF in Go
item.description: The Sequential, Concurrent, and Group Chat orchestration builders in agent-framework-go, plus wrapping a whole workflow as one nestable agent.

item.title: Capstone · DocQA — answer questions about your own documents
item.description: The capstone: a grounded DocQA agent that answers only from embedded docs via a search_docs tool, cites sources, and refuses to guess — with an optional reviewer.

item.title: Workflows with Agents — MAF in Go
item.description: Agents as Go workflow executors: switch routing, fan-out and fan-in barriers, and mixing typed function nodes with agent nodes in one graph.

item.title: A2A Server
item.description: Host one Foundry agent over A2A: newMux pins the card interface URL, wraps the agent in an a2aprovider executor, and serves the card plus JSON-RPC routes.

item.title: Workflow Mechanics — MAF in Go
item.description: The MAF workflow model in Go: executors bound to IDs, AddEdge wiring, WithOutputFrom, and typed WatchStream events - plus an upstream route-builder fix.

item.title: A2A Client
item.description: A Foundry host agent resolves remote A2A agent cards and turns each remote agent into a callable tool with agenttool.New over the a2aprovider.

item.title: Observability, Safety, and Providers — MAF in Go
item.description: Wrap every MAF run in an OpenTelemetry span, gate risky tool actions behind a permission handler, and swap Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Copilot, and Azure behind one agent.

item.title: subworkflows · Nested Order Processing
item.description: inproc.BindSubworkflowAsExecutor binds a whole workflow as one node; an order pipeline nests Payment and FraudCheck two levels deep, and inner events still bubble up.

item.title: Middleware — MAF in Go
item.description: A guardrail middleware that blocks an MAF Go run before the model, chained ahead of a logger — plus the tool-approval nil-update panic I fixed upstream in PR #472.

item.title: shared-states · Coordinating executors through shared state
item.description: One executor stores a document with QueueStateUpdate; fan-out counters read it back with ReadState, and an AddFanInBarrierEdge aggregates once both deliver. Offline.

item.title: Shaping a Run — MAF in Go
item.description: Decode typed structs from a run, drain a streamed response by ranging it, and send an image with RunMessage and DataContent.

item.title: observability · Workflow as an Agent
item.description: WithTelemetry instruments a French-to-English workflow with OpenTelemetry spans, then agentworkflow.NewAgent wraps the graph so RunText drives the whole pipeline.

item.title: Conversation and Memory — MAF in Go
item.description: A Session threads history into each run and a ContextProvider carries memory across sessions. Because a Session is JSON, both survive a process restart.

item.title: loop · A Cyclic Workflow (guess-the-number)
item.description: A workflow graph is not a DAG: two edges form a GuessNumber to Judge cycle that runs a binary search and exits only when Judge yields an output. The while-loop analogue.

item.title: Giving an Agent Tools — MAF in Go
item.description: Wrap a typed Go function as a tool with functool.MustNew, let the model call it, and compose whole agents as tools with agenttool.New.

item.title: human_in_the_loop_basic · A Workflow That Asks a Human
item.description: A RequestPort emits a RequestInfoEvent and suspends the workflow; the driver answers with SendResponse. A guess-the-number cycle shows the pause/resume mechanism.

item.title: Your First Agent — MAF in Go
item.description: The minimal MAF loop in Go: a foundryprovider agent, DefaultAzureCredential, and one RunText you either Collect or range over as a Go 1.23 iterator.

item.title: 03 · Multi-Selection Fan-Out (edge assigner)
item.description: WithEdgeAssigner yields target indexes via an iter.Seq[int], so a single message reaches several branches at once — a long email goes to both assistant and summary, offline.

item.title: Learning the Microsoft Agent Framework in Go
item.description: I learned the whole Microsoft Agent Framework in Go by building one runnable lesson per concept against Azure AI Foundry. Here is the 12-track map.

item.title: 02 · Switch Case — multi-way conditional routing
item.description: AddSwitch/AddCase/WithDefault fans one edge into three mutually-exclusive branches with a guaranteed fallback — the workflow analogue of switch/case/default, fully offline.

item.title: Hosting and the Capstone App — MAF in Python
item.description: Host MAF agents with DevUI, A2A, MCP, and AG-UI, then build DocQA — a grounded, cited multi-agent app that ties the whole Python series together.

item.title: 01 · Edge Condition (conditional edges)
item.description: The first 03-workflows lesson: AddDirectEdge attaches a func(any) bool to each edge so a spam-detection graph forks into send vs. handle-spam branches, offline.

item.title: Advanced Workflows — MAF in Python
item.description: Durable MAF workflows in Python: checkpoint and resume every superstep, suspend on request_info for a human decision, and package a workflow as an agent.

item.title: concurrent · Map-Reduce Workflow
item.description: MapReduce word-count as a five-stage workflow: fan-out to mappers, barrier to a shuffler, fan-out to reducers, barrier to completion, coordinated via shared state.

item.title: Orchestration Patterns — MAF in Python
item.description: Sequential, Concurrent, Group Chat, Handoff, Magentic — the five prebuilt MAF orchestrations in Python and when each beats hand-wiring a graph.

item.title: concurrent · Fan-out / Fan-in Workflow
item.description: A start executor broadcasts a question to two experts via a fan-out edge; a fan-in barrier edge joins both answers before the aggregator yields output.

item.title: Workflows with Agents — MAF in Python
item.description: Agents are just workflow executors: switch-case routing, fan-out/fan-in concurrency, and mixing plain function nodes with agent nodes in one graph.

item.title: checkpoint · Human-in-the-Loop with Checkpoint & Restore
item.description: A RequestPort pauses to ask a human for a guess while every super-step is checkpointed, then RestoreCheckpoint rewinds the whole graph including tries state.

item.title: Workflow Mechanics — MAF in Python
item.description: The MAF workflow model in Python: executors as nodes, edges as data flow, switch-case routing, and typed streaming events - learned model-free.

item.title: checkpoint · Checkpoint and Resume
item.description: Same guess-the-number graph, but RestoreCheckpoint rewinds the live run to the 6th snapshot and replays forward — no fresh workflow instance needed.

item.title: Observability, Safety, and Providers — MAF in Python
item.description: Turn MAF agent runs into OpenTelemetry spans, block prompt injection with information-flow control, and swap model providers behind one Agent API.

item.title: checkpoint_and_rehydrate
item.description: A cyclic guess-the-number workflow snapshots state each super-step, then a brand-new workflow instance is rehydrated from a checkpoint via ResumeStreaming.

item.title: Middleware — MAF in Python
item.description: Wrapping an MAF agent run in Python with async middleware seams — timing, logging, and a guardrail that short-circuits a tool call before it runs.

item.title: workflow_as_an_agent · A Workflow, Wrapped as One Agent
item.description: A concurrent French+English workflow is hosted as one agent via agentworkflow.NewAgent, with IncludeOutputsInResponse surfacing the merged output.

item.title: Shaping a Run — MAF in Python
item.description: Three dials on one run() call: typed results via response_format, consuming a stream event by event, and sending an image alongside text.
